description: Create test/Router.Access.t.sol covering FR-13/14: Ownable2Step ownership transfer (two-step accept), separate liquidator address settable only by owner, Pausable behavior blocking all swap entry points, and sweep functions (transferRouterFunds + swapRouterFunds) being callable only by owner OR liquidator. Asserts that the liquidator cannot call config functions (setPendingExecutor, setLiquidator, pause/unpause, transferOwnership).
commit: test(router): add access control, pause, and sweep permission tests
steps: Create test/Router.Access.t.sol with setUp that deploys Router with a dedicated owner EOA and a dedicated liquidator EOA; deploys ExecutionProxy and wires it via two-step. Seed router with protocol-fee dust by running one successful swap.

Ownable2Step tests: test_Ownable_InitialOwner (owner() == _owner); test_Ownable_TransferTwoStep (transferOwnership sets pendingOwner; acceptOwnership must be called by new owner; only then does owner() change); test_Ownable_NonOwner_CannotSetPendingExecutor; test_Ownable_NonOwner_CannotSetLiquidator; test_Ownable_NonOwner_CannotPause.

Liquidator tests: test_Liquidator_Initial (liquidator() == deployed value); test_Liquidator_OwnerCanUpdate (setLiquidator(newAddr) emits LiquidatorUpdated, getter returns newAddr); test_Liquidator_CannotSelfUpdate (vm.prank(liquidator); expect Unauthorized); test_Liquidator_CannotCallConfig (setPendingExecutor, setLiquidator, pause all revert Unauthorized/OwnableUnauthorizedAccount for liquidator).

Pause tests: test_Pause_BlocksSwap (pause; user calls swap; reverts with Pausable Router.Paused.selector); test_Pause_BlocksSwapMulti; test_Pause_DoesNotBlockSweep (transferRouterFunds works while paused); test_Pause_Unpause_Restores (unpause; swap works again); test_Pause_NonOwner_CannotPause.

Sweep tests: test_TransferRouterFunds_ByOwner (sweeps ERC20 + native ETH with mixed tokens array to dest; emits FundsTransferred); test_TransferRouterFunds_ByLiquidator (same, but called by liquidator); test_TransferRouterFunds_ByStranger_Reverts (Unauthorized); test_TransferRouterFunds_ArrayLengthMismatch_Reverts; test_TransferRouterFunds_ZeroDest_Reverts (ZeroAddress); test_TransferRouterFunds_EmptyArrays_NoOp (no revert, balances unchanged); test_SwapRouterFunds_ByOwner (sweeps one token into another via executor; uses router-held balance as input, not msg.sender's); test_SwapRouterFunds_ByStranger_Reverts (Unauthorized).
Run `forge test --match-path test/Router.Access.t.sol` and confirm all pass.
acceptance_criteria: [auto] File test/Router.Access.t.sol exists; `forge test --match-path test/Router.Access.t.sol` passes.
[auto] grep -n 'transferOwnership\|acceptOwnership' test/Router.Access.t.sol returns at least 2 matches (proving 2-step tested end-to-end).
[manual] test_TransferRouterFunds_ByOwner exercises both an ERC20 and the NATIVE_ETH_SENTINEL entry in the tokens array and asserts dest.balance delta > 0 as well as IERC20.balanceOf(dest) delta > 0.
[auto] All tests pass; zero skipped; `forge test --match-path test/Router.Access.t.sol -v`.
[manual] Liquidator negative-path tests prove the role is strictly narrower than owner (no governance capabilities).
implementation_notes: Added test/Router.Access.t.sol with 22 passing tests covering FR-13/14: Ownable2Step (initial owner, two-step transfer end-to-end, non-owner reverts on setPendingExecutor/setLiquidator/pause), liquidator role (initial value, owner-only update, cannot self-update, cannot call any config surface), Pausable (blocks swap + swapMulti, does not block sweep, unpause restores, non-owner cannot pause), transferRouterFunds (owner + liquidator happy paths with ERC20+native mixed array, stranger Unauthorized, ArrayLengthMismatch, ZeroAddress, empty-arrays no-op), and swapRouterFunds (owner sweeps router-held dust into another token via executor; stranger reverts Unauthorized). setUp seeds 10e18 tokenA dust by running one 1%-protocol-fee swap. Commit 1c89682.
